AgentBudgetExceededEnvelope.description: per-session `max_total_llm_calls` budget exceeded. The session is tombstoned atomically inside `llm_provider.dispatch`; subsequent `/v1/agent` calls against the same session_id return 404 (session ended). Header `X-GhostCrawl-Warning: budget-exceeded` is copied to the actual HTTP response by the serving gateway.

session_viewer_url.description: Live session viewer URL when configured.

profile_id.description: Optional stable profile identifier. When supplied on a successful sync `/v1/agent` call, the executed step trace is recorded into the in-process action cache (keyed by `(profile_id, task)`) and can be replayed via `POST /v1/agent/action-cache/replay`. Response payload includes `action_cache_recorded: true|false` so callers can confirm cache durability.

task.description: Agent task payload; URL fields are policy-validated when present.

BatchScrapeRequest.description: Request body for POST /v1/scrape/batch. Accepts 1–20 URLs and a concurrency cap 1–10. All other fields mirror the shared scrape params from ScrapeBody and are applied uniformly to every URL in the batch.
BatchScrapeRequest.title: BatchScrapeRequest

Retry-After.description: Integer seconds (RFC 9110 sec 10. 2. 4); see components.headers. RetryAfter for ladder.

reason.description: Coarse reason ("limiter_reject"). intentionally omits limits.reason to avoid leaking limiter internal state.

route.description: Route path that rejected the request.

ContentRequest.description: POST /v1/content request body, render a URL and return its content.

engine.description: Engine override (content works on every engine).
engine.title: Engine

identity_country.description: Two-letter country code to pin the exit country.
identity_country.title: Identity Country

proxy.description: Override proxy URL for this request.
proxy.title: Proxy

url.description: URL to render and return content for.
url.title: Url

ContentRequest.title: ContentRequest

routing_mode.description: Routing mode. auto (default) = we pick the cheapest network that succeeds and automatically escalate on a block. standard = normal targets only. premium = always use our premium network. Most callers should leave this at auto.
routing_mode.title: Routing Mode

EngineFidelityReceipt.description: Per-response Engine Fidelity Receipt, a diagnostic (not-warranty) attestation of which browser engine served the request. Mirrored in the `X-GhostCrawl-Receipt` response header.

value.detail: startup config-preflight is required and current verdict is degraded

ttl_seconds.description: New TTL in seconds (30s. 24h). Omit for the store default.
ttl_seconds.title: Ttl Seconds

ExtractRequest.description: POST /v1/extract request body, schema-driven extraction (deterministic) + bring-your-own-model extraction. Two extraction modes: - Deterministic (default, model_provider absent): CSS/regex/keyword extraction. Requires 'schema'. Returns {"url","data","token_estimate"}. - Bring-your-own-model (model_provider present): GhostCrawl fetches and prepares clean Markdown; your connected model performs the semantic extraction (you are not billed for model inference here, only the request quota). Returns {"urls","data","token_estimate"}. Security notes: - The 'schema' field is validated before any fetch (fail-fast input guard). - URLs are validated against private, loopback, and metadata targets before any fetch. - Error responses never echo the schema body, fetched content, or model credentials. - model_provider.api_key is request-scoped only: never persisted, logged, or returned in any response body or error message.

behavior_actions.description: BYO behavior: trusted-input actions replayed on the loaded page instead of the built-in human session. Sanitized + bounded server-side (max 256 actions). Available on every tier.
behavior_actions.title: Behavior Actions

engine.description: Engine override: auto (default) | chrome | firefox | webkit.
engine.title: Engine

identity_country.description: Optional two-letter country code to pin (geo-filters the exit and derives timezone; tier-gated). Omitted = timezone follows the exit.
identity_country.title: Identity Country

language.description: Optional browser language tag (e.g. 'en-US','es-ES'). Sets the identity's locale and language headers coherently, independent of the exit country. Available on every tier.
language.title: Language

model_provider.description: BYO model credentials dict: {type, api_key, model, base_url?}. When present → BYO-LLM extraction path (GhostCrawl bills the request, not the inference tokens, inference runs on the user's connected model). When absent → deterministic CSS/regex/keyword extraction (default).
model_provider.title: Model Provider

prompt.description: Free-form extraction instruction for BYO-LLM mode. Allowed without a schema (schema-less prose extraction). Ignored in deterministic mode (model_provider absent).
prompt.title: Prompt

schema.description: JSON Schema describing the desired extraction output. Required for deterministic extraction (model_provider absent). Optional for BYO-LLM extraction (model_provider present), when provided, the LLM is instructed to respond in JSON-mode conforming to this schema.
schema.title: Schema

url.description: URL to fetch and extract from (single-URL mode). Required when 'urls' is not provided.
url.title: Url

urls.description: Multi-URL cross-page aggregation (bring-your-own-model mode only). Each URL is fetched and converted to clean Markdown, then concatenated with per-URL delimiter headers; one aggregated model call extracts across all pages. Bounded to ≤10 URLs. Cannot be combined with 'url' when both are provided.
urls.title: Urls

ExtractRequest.title: ExtractRequest

MapBody.description: Firecrawl-compatible /v1/map request body. naming convention warnings for intentional camelCase).
MapBody.title: MapBody

PdfRequest.description: POST /v1/pdf request body, render a URL to PDF. Renders the target URL to a PDF document. Supported on Chrome identities; requests that resolve to a Firefox or WebKit identity return 400 pdf_engine_unsupported. Security: - The URL is validated against private/loopback/metadata targets. - All traffic egresses through your configured proxy. - Subject to your daily request quota.

engine.description: Engine override. PDF is chrome-only; 'firefox' or 'webkit' returns 400 pdf_engine_unsupported immediately (before dispatch).
engine.title: Engine

identity_country.description: Two-letter country code to pin the exit country.
identity_country.title: Identity Country

proxy.description: Override proxy URL for this request.
proxy.title: Proxy

PdfRequest.title: PdfRequest

ProblemDetails.description: Returned on a non-2xx response when the request could not be completed on our side (authentication, rate limiting, capacity, render timeout, …). Sent with the `application/problem+json` media type (RFC 9457).

type.description: A stable URI that identifies the error type. Dereferences to the matching entry on the public error reference.

title.description: A short, human-readable summary of the error type.

status.description: The HTTP status code for this response.

detail.description: A human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence.

instance.description: The request identifier for this occurrence (the same value as the `X-Request-Id` response header). Quote it when contacting support.

retryable.description: Whether retrying the same call (after a short backoff) can succeed. `false` means the request will keep failing until you change something.

retry_after.description: Suggested number of seconds to wait before retrying. Present only on retryable codes; mirrors the `Retry-After` response header when set.

ProfileCreateRequest.description: POST /v1/profiles body, create a named persistent profile.

identity_spec.description: Public IdentityRequest spec (operating system, browser, device, viewport, locale, and timezone). The profile mints a STABLE deterministic identity_id from it, so every scrape with profile=<name> rehydrates the SAME fingerprint. When omitted, a default desktop-chrome identity is minted.
identity_spec.title: Identity Spec

name.description: Profile name. Unique per org (409 on conflict).
name.title: Name

ProfileCreateRequest.title: ProfileCreateRequest

ProfileUpdateRequest.description: PUT /v1/profiles/{name} body, rename and/or rebind storage_state.

storage_state_id.description: Rebind storage_state id. Pass null to keep; use empty string '' to clear.
storage_state_id.title: Storage State Id

ProfileUpdateRequest.title: ProfileUpdateRequest

server.description: Proxy URL with scheme and host:port.

RetryDeliveryResponse.description: Response for POST /v1/webhooks/{id}/deliveries/{delivery_id}/retry (202 Accepted). The original event_id is preserved so downstream consumers can detect the retry; a fresh delivery_id is assigned for the new attempt.
RetryDeliveryResponse.title: RetryDeliveryResponse

RotateSecretResponse.description: Response for POST /v1/webhooks/{id}/rotate-secret. `secret` is the new signing secret. The previous secret remains valid for a short grace period so in-flight deliveries can still be verified. `prev_secret_expires_at` is when the grace period ends.

prev_secret_expires_at.description: When the previous signing secret expires and the grace period ends.
prev_secret_expires_at.title: Prev Secret Expires At

secret.description: New signing secret. The previous secret remains valid during the grace period.
secret.title: Secret

RotateSecretResponse.title: RotateSecretResponse

monitor_mode.description: When True, fires notify_webhook only when content hash changes.
monitor_mode.title: Monitor Mode

name.description: Schedule name. Unique per org (409 on conflict).
name.title: Name

notify_webhook.description: HTTPS webhook URL for change-monitor notifications.
notify_webhook.title: Notify Webhook

ScrapePageResult.description: One per-URL scrape result inside ScrapeResult.results. ``extra="allow"`` because the fleet/ attaches additional diagnostic members (headers, resolved_url, identity_id, result_error,.) that vary by engine + path; the documented core is what every SDK reads.

result_error.description: Per-item TARGET error {code, retryable} when the fetch failed.
result_error.title: Result Error

status_code.description: Upstream HTTP status of the fetch.
status_code.title: Status Code

ScrapePageResult.title: ScrapePageResult

batch_identity_mode.description: "persist" (default): same identity for all URLs in a batch. "randomize": fresh identity per URL (each counts against quota).
batch_identity_mode.title: Batch Identity Mode

behavior_actions.description: BYO behavior: a list of trusted-input actions replayed on the loaded page instead of the built-in human session. Each item is {"kind":"move","x":int,"y":int} | {"kind":"wheel","x":int,"y":int,"dy":int} | {"kind":"dwell","ms":int}. Sanitized + bounded server-side (max 256 actions). Available on every tier.
behavior_actions.title: Behavior Actions

chunk_tokens.description: Maximum tokens per markdown chunk. Clamped server-side to [256, 32768] regardless of caller input.
chunk_tokens.title: Chunk Tokens

engine.description: Engine override. 'auto' (default): the identity's fingerprint decides the engine. 'chrome'|'firefox'|'webkit': force that engine, enforced only if coherent with the identity (e.g. iOS identities are always webkit); incoherent picks are rejected, never coerced to a hybrid.
engine.title: Engine

format.description: Response format. 'html' (default): the fully rendered DOM. 'markdown': returns a {markdown, chunks, citations, token_estimate} envelope with prompt-injection-neutralized, token-budgeted output. 'pdf': returns application/pdf bytes (Chrome identities only; Firefox and WebKit return 400 pdf_engine_unsupported).
format.title: Format

full_page.description: When screenshot=true, capture the FULL document height instead of just the visible viewport. Ignored when screenshot_selector is set.
full_page.title: Full Page

identity_country.description: Optional two-letter country code (e.g. 'US','DE'). When set, the exit is geo-filtered to that country and the identity's timezone is derived from it. When omitted, the timezone follows the rotating exit country. Language is independent, see the `language` field.
identity_country.title: Identity Country

identity_spec.description: Inline identity specification. Creates an ephemeral identity.
identity_spec.title: Identity Spec

include_citations.description: When format=markdown, include deduplicated citation list {url, anchor_text} from the scraped page.
include_citations.title: Include Citations

language.description: Optional browser language tag (e.g. 'en-US','es-ES','de-DE'). Sets the identity's locale and language headers coherently, independent of the exit country. When omitted, the identity's default locale is used. Available on every tier.
language.title: Language

profile.description: Named persistent profile (multi-accounting). Resolves to a durable identity_id (→ same deterministic fingerprint) + optional saved storage_state (→ cookies/localStorage) for the calling org. Reuses the exact identity+cookies on every request, with no 30-minute sticky-session expiry. 404 if the named profile does not exist for the org.
profile.title: Profile

proxy.description: Bring-your-own proxy URL. Schemes: socks5, socks5h, http, https. Any credentials in the URL are redacted from logs. Example: socks5://user:pass@host:1080.
proxy.title: Proxy

routing_mode.description: Routing mode. auto (default) = we pick the cheapest network that succeeds and automatically escalate on a block. standard = normal targets only. premium = always use our premium network. Most callers should leave this at auto.
routing_mode.title: Routing Mode

screenshot.description: When true, a screenshot is captured during the fetch and returned as a base64-encoded PNG in result['screenshot']. Off by default.
screenshot.title: Screenshot

screenshot_selector.description: When screenshot=true, clip the capture to the first DOM element matching this CSS selector (element screenshot). Takes precedence over full_page.
screenshot_selector.title: Screenshot Selector

session.description: Sticky-session token (paid tiers). Requests sharing a token reuse the same identity → same exit + cookies (returning-visitor coherence). Bounded TTL; opaque [A-Za-z0-9._-], max 128 chars.
session.title: Session

stream.description: When true, returns text/event-stream with one SSE event per chunk. Per-tenant concurrency is capped (default 5). Final 'done' event carries estimated_llm_input_tokens.
stream.title: Stream

timeout_ms.description: Optional per-request timeout in milliseconds. Clamped server-side to ≤120000 ms (120s); values outside [0, 120000] are rejected (422).
timeout_ms.title: Timeout Ms

ScrapeResult.description: POST /v1/scrape 200 response body. Documents the customer-facing shape so the OpenAPI 200 schema is no longer an empty ``{}`` (Kiota/typed SDKs need a named schema). ``extra="allow"`` keeps it forward-compatible with the markdown envelope (``format``/``markdown``/ ``chunks``/``citations``/``token_estimate``/``estimated_llm_input_tokens``) and the html envelope (``results``/``identity_id``/``identity_ids``/ ``identities_used``) without over-constraining the route (which returns a JSONResponse, so this model documents but does not coerce the body).

format.description: Output format when a non-default format was requested.
format.title: Format

status.description: Aggregate run status (e.g. completed / partial / failed).
status.title: Status

token_estimate.description: Estimated token count of the markdown output.
token_estimate.title: Token Estimate

ScrapeResult.title: ScrapeResult

ScreenshotRequest.description: POST /v1/screenshot request body, capture a URL to a PNG screenshot.

engine.description: Engine override (screenshot works on every engine).
engine.title: Engine

full_page.description: Capture the FULL document height instead of just the viewport.
full_page.title: Full Page

identity_country.description: Two-letter country code to pin the exit country.
identity_country.title: Identity Country

proxy.description: Override proxy URL for this request.
proxy.title: Proxy

screenshot_selector.description: Clip to the first DOM element matching this CSS selector (element screenshot).
screenshot_selector.title: Screenshot Selector

ScreenshotRequest.title: ScreenshotRequest

country.description: Optional 2-letter ISO country code for geo-filter. Tavily v1 ignores this silently.
country.title: Country

engine.description: Search engine to use: 'brave', 'tavily', 'google', 'bing', or 'yandex'. May also be passed via X-GhostCrawl-Search-Engine header.
engine.title: Engine

extra_params.description: Vertical-specific extras (google only). jobs: location; scholar: year_low/year_high; play: mode/app_id; reviews: place_id. Ignored by brave/tavily.
extra_params.title: Extra Params

freshness.description: Optional freshness filter (adapter-specific; Brave: 'pd'/'pw'/'pm'/'py' for day/week/month/year).
freshness.title: Freshness

vertical.description: Engine-specific vertical (google only): 'search' (default) | 'jobs' | 'scholar' | 'play' | 'reviews'. Ignored by brave/tavily.
vertical.title: Vertical

profile.description: Rehydrates the saved identity bundle for this session, same deterministic fingerprint as /v1/scrape with profile=<name>.
profile.title: Profile

profile_id.description: Profile to attach this storage state to (sticky per).
profile_id.title: Profile Id

profile_id.description: Profile whose active_storage_state_id binding to clear.
profile_id.title: Profile Id

allow_private_targets.description: Allow private/internal IP targets (hosted mode only).
allow_private_targets.title: Allow Private Targets

event_types.description: Event types to subscribe to. Must be non-empty subset of: ['agent.run.completed', 'agent.run.failed', 'budget.exceeded', 'budget.warning', 'cost.daily.threshold_critical', 'cost.daily.threshold_warning', 'cost.month_end.projected_overrun', 'crawl.completed', 'dataset.updated', 'extract.completed', 'extract.failed', 'manual_test', 'recording.saved', 'scrape.completed', 'scrape.failed', 'session.ended', 'team.api_key.created', 'team.api_key.revoked', 'team.signup.verified'].
event_types.title: Event Types

url.description: Destination URL for webhook delivery. Must be HTTPS.
url.title: Url

WebhookCreateResponse.description: Response for POST /v1/webhooks (201 Created). `secret` is the plaintext signing secret returned ONCE. Store it immediately, subsequent GET calls return WebhookPublic which excludes the secret.

secret.description: Plaintext signing secret. Returned only once, store it now.
secret.title: Secret

WebhookCreateResponse.title: WebhookCreateResponse

WebhookDeliveryPublic.description: Wire-public delivery log representation. 13 wire-public fields per webhook_deliveries schema. All fields are operator/integrator-facing, no secret material.
WebhookDeliveryPublic.title: WebhookDeliveryPublic

WebhookPublic.description: Public webhook subscription details. The signing secret is never included in list or get responses, it is returned only at creation time or after a secret rotation.
WebhookPublic.title: WebhookPublic

ErrorCode.description: Stable, machine-readable error code. The same vocabulary is used across every endpoint and every SDK. Codes are grouped into two channels: codes returned on a non-2xx response (a problem on our side) and codes returned inside a 200 response body (a problem reaching the target).

ResultError.description: Present inside a 200 response when the request ran but the target site could not be retrieved successfully (an HTTP error from the site, an anti-bot wall, a CAPTCHA, an unreachable host, or no extractable content). The result also carries `ok: false`.

retryable.description: Whether retrying could succeed. For a blocked page or CAPTCHA this is `true` and means retry with a different identity; for a definitive client error from the site it is `false`.

target_status.description: The HTTP status the target site returned, when known (for example `404` or `503`).

reason.description: An optional sub-reason giving more detail (for example a connection or name-resolution failure when the host could not be reached).

bearerAuth.description: Paste your API key as: Authorization: Bearer gck_YOUR_KEY_HERE

BadRequest.description: The request was malformed or a parameter was invalid.

Unauthorized.description: Authentication is required: the API key is missing or invalid.

PaymentRequired.description: A usage or spend limit was reached.

Forbidden.description: The API key does not have permission for this action.

NotFound.description: The requested resource was not found.

RateLimited.description: The rate limit was exceeded. Wait for the number of seconds in `Retry-After`, then retry.

ServiceUnavailable.description: The service is temporarily unavailable (capacity, a transient render fault, or a busy page). Retryable: back off and retry.

GatewayTimeout.description: The request was not completed in time. Retryable: back off and retry.

InternalError.description: An unexpected error occurred on our side.

RetryAfter.description: Seconds to wait before retrying after a 429 (capacity exhausted) or 503 response.

info.description: The GhostCrawl web data API: scrape, crawl, search, and extract any URL with real browser engines and consistent identity profiles.
info.title: GhostCrawl API

400.description: Task policy failed, task URL failed gateway policy, proxy failed policy, or tenant/workspace proxy constraint

404.description: Returned when the agent capability is not enabled for this account or deployment. The agent lane runs server-side orchestration, the navigate / act / observe steps execute on GhostCrawl's managed browser fleet, and calls your bring-your-own (BYO) LLM for the reasoning step via the `X-LLM-Api-Key` / `X-LLM-Provider` request headers. While the capability is not enabled the route returns 404 (indistinguishable from a non-existent route, for; once enabled it returns the agent execution envelope. The BYO LLM is the only part you supply, the orchestration itself is hosted.

429.description: Either capacity exhausted (Retry-After header present, CapacityExhausted429 body) or per-session max_total_llm_calls budget exceeded (X-GhostCrawl-Warning: budget-exceeded).

503.description: Startup config-preflight gate blocked request handling

post.description: Engine-agnostic live-view frame capture (cross-engine). Returns a single base64-encoded JPEG frame of a live interactive session, using the same capture path across Chromium, Firefox, and WebKit. Gating: 1. Tenant authentication 2. Revocation check 3. 4. Session ownership (the session must belong to the authenticated organisation) Returns 200 {"format":"jpeg","data":"<base64>","engine","width","height"} on success; 403 cdp_not_enabled (tier) / cdp_revoked; 404 session_not_found; 502 frame_failed.
Mint a short-TTL signed URL for cloud CDP access. Returns 200 {"url": "wss://.", "expires_in_seconds": 120} on success.
post.summary: Get a live CDP endpoint URL

post.description: render a URL and return its content as a JSON envelope. Equivalent to /v1/scrape returning the rendered content field. Gated exactly like /v1/scrape (daily request quota + GB egress cap); works on every engine.
post.summary: Render a URL and return its content

get.description: cursor pagination. {items, next_cursor, total_count}. change in; created_at_from > created_at_to → 400 (not 422). Error envelope for 403 unchanged (at error layer).

schema.description: Idempotency key (8–256 ASCII printable chars). Supply a fresh UUID or ULID per logically-distinct request. Replaying the same request with the same key returns the cached response without re-execution. Same key + different body → 409 idempotency_key_reused_with_different_body. Body > 1 MiB with this header → 413.

get.description: status, plus OPT-IN long-poll. Default (``wait`` absent/false): instant status snapshot, behavior unchanged. ``?wait=true&timeout_s=N``: BLOCKS event-driven (same cross-worker `` signal as start-and-wait) until the run reaches a terminal state (completed|cancelled|failed|failed_resumable) or ``timeout_s`` elapses, THEN returns the run record (results included when completed). A timeout returns the CURRENT non-terminal run as HTTP 200, a long-poll, NOT an error. ``timeout_s`` defaults to 300 and is capped at a server-configured maximum (default 600).

schema.description: Idempotency key (8–256 ASCII printable chars). Supply a fresh UUID or ULID per logically-distinct request. Replaying the same request with the same key returns the cached response without re-execution. Same key + different body → 409 idempotency_key_reused_with_different_body. Body > 1 MiB with this header → 413.

Idempotency key (8–256 ASCII printable chars). Supply a fresh UUID or ULID per logically-distinct request. Replaying the same request with the same key returns the cached response without re-execution. Same key + different body → 409 idempotency_key_reused_with_different_body. Body > 1 MiB with this header → 413.

post.description: queue an async deep crawl. Returns immediately with run_id and status="queued". Webhook fires on completion when webhook_url is provided. Billing: crawl_pages_delta (Option A).

schema.description: Idempotency key (8–256 ASCII printable chars). Supply a fresh UUID or ULID per logically-distinct request. Replaying the same request with the same key returns the cached response without re-execution. Same key + different body → 409 idempotency_key_reused_with_different_body. Body > 1 MiB with this header → 413.

get.description: poll deep crawl job status. Returns current status and result count. Results array included when status = "completed".
cursor pagination. (created_at, id) base64 tuple; created_at_from > created_at_to → 400 (not 422).

post.description: register a new dataset sink. Credentials are stored encrypted and are never returned in the response. Duplicate names return 400 dataset_name_conflict. Success returns 201 with dataset metadata.

delete.description: delete. Returns 200 on success, 404 on miss.

get.description: metadata only. Credentials are never included in the response.
paginated rows from a dataset. Returns {rows: [{row_id, written_at, payload}], next_cursor}.

post.description: append rows to an existing dataset. Returns {appended: N} on success. Rejects when rows is not a list of objects (422) or exceeds the per-request cap (413).
post.summary: Append rows to a dataset (landing zone)

get.description: GET /v1/downloads/{id} → the download's signed retrieval link / metadata. we do not confirm existence of another tenant's artifact).

post.description: structured extraction (deterministic default) + bring-your-own-model extraction. Two modes (selected by model_provider): DETERMINISTIC (model_provider absent, default): 1. The schema is validated (input guard). 2. The URL is validated against private/loopback/metadata targets. 3. The page is fetched and rendered. 4. Structured data is extracted via CSS/regex/keyword rules. 5. The result is validated against the schema, 422 on mismatch (field path only). Returns {"url","data","token_estimate"}. BRING-YOUR-OWN-MODEL (model_provider present): 1. Target URLs are resolved (urls or [url]). 2. The schema is validated if present. 3. At least one of prompt or schema is required, 400 prompt_or_schema_required otherwise. 4. Model credentials are validated, 400 invalid_model_provider on error (the message never echoes the key). 5. Every target URL is validated before fetching, 400 ssrf_blocked. 6. Each URL is fetched and converted to clean Markdown. 7. Your connected model performs the extraction. 8. With a schema present, the result is validated, 422 on mismatch. Returns {"urls","data","token_estimate"}. Security: 401 without a valid Bearer token. - model_provider.api_key is request-scoped: never persisted, logged, or echoed. - Error responses never echo the schema body, fetched content, or credentials.

schema.description: Idempotency key (8–256 ASCII printable chars). Supply a fresh UUID or ULID per logically-distinct request. Replaying the same request with the same key returns the cached response without re-execution. Same key + different body → 409 idempotency_key_reused_with_different_body. Body > 1 MiB with this header → 413.

schema.title: Response Saas Extract V1 Extract Post

delete.description: remove a KV entry. Returns {deleted: true}. 404 on miss).

get.description: read a KV entry. Returns {key, value, value_type}. For blobs, returns bytes with application/octet-stream content-type. 404 on miss).

put.description: write or overwrite a KV entry. Body: {"value": <any>, "type": "json"|"text"|"blob", "ttl_at"?: <iso8601>} Returns {key, value_type, size}. 413 on oversize.

post.description: sitemap URL discovery. 1. The seed URL is validated against private/loopback/metadata targets. 2. The request is quota-checked. 3. URLs are discovered from the site's sitemaps. 4. Results are filtered to the same registrable domain by default. 5. An optional case-insensitive substring filter is applied to URLs. 6. Results are capped at 10000 regardless of the requested limit.
Return ``page.content`` for the live session, capped at 5 MB. callers retry with finer scope or fall back to /v1/page/eval for selector-rooted extraction.

Run caller-supplied JavaScript in the pinned page context. All execution paths (disabled, timeout-killed, result-capped, PII-filtered) are audit-logged. A pre-execution audit record is written before the watchdog fires so interrupted evaluations remain traceable, paired with a final success/failure/denied record sharing the same ``eval_id`` correlation key. The result payload is never written to the audit log, only its size and hash.
post.summary: Evaluate JavaScript on the pinned page

post.description: Return the HAR-equivalent JSON for the live session. Captures all network traffic recorded during the session and returns it in HAR format. Responses over 5 MB return a truncation flag in the body rather than an error.
post.summary: Capture HAR for the live session
Upload a file to a file input

post.description: render a URL to PDF. Equivalent to /v1/scrape with format=pdf. Returns application/pdf bytes. Supported on Chrome identities; Firefox and WebKit return 400. The URL is validated and all traffic egresses through your configured proxy. Requires a paid plan and is subject to your request quota (same as /v1/extract).

get.description: cursor pagination. new endpoint. silently created_at_from > created_at_to → 400 (not 422).

post.description: mint a durable {org, name} → identity binding. 409 on duplicate name; 422 on invalid identity_spec.

delete.description: remove a named binding. 204 on success, 404 on miss.

get.description: fetch a named binding (404 if absent for org).
get.summary: Get a named profile by name

put.description: rename and/or rebind storage_state. The identity_id is IMMUTABLE (changing it would silently change the fingerprint and break multi-accounting coherence). 404 if absent; 409 on rename collision.
put.summary: Rename a profile and/or rebind its storage_state

get.description: cursor pagination. items[*] shape unchanged. created_at_from > created_at_to → 400 (not 422).
Get the HLS-style manifest pointer for a visual recording. Returns 200 {manifest_url, frame_count, status, engine} or 404 for
List paginated frame references for frame-by-frame replay. next_cursor == -1 means no more pages.

post.description: Start a visual recording for a live session. Creates a session_recordings row. Returns 201 with the recording row id. Security gates (in order): 1. 2. 3. ownership gate: session ownership verified
Stop a visual recording and finalize (assemble m3u8 manifest). Returns 200 with manifest_ref.

get.description: list.

post.description: create a cron-triggered scrape/crawl schedule. Validations: cron_expr must parse (422 invalid_cron) job_type must be 'scrape', 'crawl', or 'change_monitor' (422 invalid_job_type) notify_webhook must be SSRF-safe HTTPS URL if provided (422 invalid_webhook) per-org count < cap (429 schedule_cap_reached) unique name per org (409 schedule_exists)

get.description: synthetic run history from last_run_at. Returns a list of run summary objects derived from the schedule's stored last_run_at + last_result_hash. A schedule that has never fired returns an empty list. One that has fired returns a single summary with status='completed'. Status values: 'completed' (ran and produced a hash), 'pending' (never ran). This endpoint allows callers to poll for schedule execution without a separate run-history table, the writes last_run_at on every successful fire.
get.summary: List run history for a schedule

post.description: Bearer-gated scrape with identity field. Accepts: ``identity: "id_."`` Re-use a persisted identity (no quota charge). - ``identity_spec: {.}`` Inline identity creation (counts against quota). - neither Host-identity fallback; warns in response. - Both → 422 invalid_request. is unreachable from this route, profile.identity_id is always populated. stream=True branch returns text/event-stream with per-tenant cap.

schema.description: Idempotency key (8–256 ASCII printable chars). Supply a fresh UUID or ULID per logically-distinct request. Replaying the same request with the same key returns the cached response without re-execution. Same key + different body → 409 idempotency_key_reused_with_different_body. Body > 1 MiB with this header → 413.

Gc-Initial-Status-Code.description: Upstream HTTP status of the initial fetch (string-encoded integer).

Gc-Resolved-Url.description: Final URL after redirects; falls back to the requested URL.

default.description: Error response. Our-side failures use this `application/problem+json` body; target-side failures are returned inside the 200 result as `result_error`.

schema.description: When the request ran but the target could not be retrieved, the 200 result carries this object.

post.description: concurrent URL scrape (max 20 URLs, max concurrency 10). 4-16 processes each URL with authentication. Each URL is processed (batch_identity_mode="persist" → one identity is created and reused for every URL). DoS mitigations (/02):

schema.description: Idempotency key (8–256 ASCII printable chars). Supply a fresh UUID or ULID per logically-distinct request. Replaying the same request with the same key returns the cached response without re-execution. Same key + different body → 409 idempotency_key_reused_with_different_body. Body > 1 MiB with this header → 413.

post.description: capture a URL to a PNG and return the raw image/png bytes. Equivalent to /v1/scrape with screenshot=true. Gated exactly like /v1/scrape (daily request quota + GB egress cap); works on every engine. Mirrors /v1/pdf's binary-response path but returns image/png.
post.summary: Capture a URL to a PNG screenshot

get.description: Fetch a screenshot blob by UUID ref, owner-scoped. Returns: 200 ``{ref, png_base64, page_url_host, created_at}`` on success. 404 ``{code: "not_found", message: "."}`` for missing ref OR enumeration of blobs belonging to other organisations.
get.summary: Fetch stored screenshot blob by UUID ref

post.description: multi-backend search. Engine selection: ``engine`` body field or ``X-GhostCrawl-Search-Engine`` header. Auth: tenant bearer + customer's search API key via ``X-Provider-Authorization: Bearer <key>`` (never persisted).

schema.description: Idempotency key (8–256 ASCII printable chars). Supply a fresh UUID or ULID per logically-distinct request. Replaying the same request with the same key returns the cached response without re-execution. Same key + different body → 409 idempotency_key_reused_with_different_body. Body > 1 MiB with this header → 413.

get.description: 200 + SearchResult when done (real result items = GREEN); 202 {status:"running"} while the extended rotation is still going (carrying ``deadline``/``poll_max_seconds`` so the client loop is bounded); 200 {status:"open", items:[]} on honest exhaustion (challenged/0-result, never a faked GREEN); 200 {status:"temporarily_unavailable"} when the deadline passes with the record still ``running`` (lost worker, retryable terminal); 404 if unknown/expired/foreign. Ownership-scoped: a job created by another tenant reads as 404. BOUNDED-TERMINAL CONTRACT (no infinite poll): every poll resolves to a terminal within the stamped deadline REGARDLESS of worker health. A 404 is TERMINAL, the job expired or is unknown; the query may be retried at a HUMAN pace after Retry-After, but a 404 must NEVER be auto-interpreted as "resubmit the same query in a tight loop" (that is the one behavior that turns a recoverable miss into an infinite loop). See the SDK/MCP wrapper.

parameters.description: Search job id from a 202 /v1/search handoff.
parameters.name: job_id

schema.description: Search job id from a 202 /v1/search handoff.
schema.title: Job Id

put.description: **Self-host deployments only.** Validate + persist a BYO behavior JSON, then report the sanitized count. Contract: ``{"human_actions": [ {kind,.},. ]}``. ``human_actions`` MUST be a list; it is passed through byo_behavior.sanitize_actions (allow-list + clamp). A non-empty input that sanitizes to an EMPTY plan (every action an invalid kind) → 422 invalid_behavior_json (nothing valid to apply). Success → write ~/.ghostcrawl/behavior.json + {reloaded, actions}.
put.summary: Self-host only — set the bring-your-own behavior configuration

put.description: **Self-host deployments only.** Validate + persist a BYO identity JSON, then reload the provider. Contract: the JSON MUST contain all of byo_identity._REQUIRED_KEYS (8 keys). Missing any → 422 invalid_identity_json (no write). Success → write to ``<identities_dir>/<name>.json`` (name from an optional ``name`` field, else a single ``byo`` slot) + reload via LocalIdentityProvider → {reloaded}.
put.summary: Self-host only — set the bring-your-own identity profile

put.description: **Self-host deployments only.** Validate + persist a BYO proxy JSON document, then reload the provider. Contract: ``{"proxies": [ {url, operating system,.},. ]}``. Each entry MUST carry a ``url`` and ``operating system`` (CLAUDE.md residential-proxy rule). Malformed → 422 invalid_proxy_json (no write). Success → write to the proxies.json target + reload via LocalProxyProvider → {reloaded, count}.
put.summary: Self-host only — set the bring-your-own proxy configuration

get.description: cursor-paginated listing of active sessions. Sessions created by scrape workers and via the API are unified in the same listing, all sessions for your organisation are visible. Results are strictly; no cross-organisation sessions are returned.

post.description: explicit session creation with engine preference. Accepts engine in {chrome, firefox, webkit, auto}.
POST /v1/sessions/{profile_id}/recording/start. Tier: interaction (recording_enabled on org). Returns 201 RecordingResponse;
post.summary: Start an action recording for a session

post.description: POST /v1/sessions/{profile_id}/recording/stop. Tier: interaction. Idempotent if already finalized, returns 200 with current status.
post.summary: Stop and finalize an active recording

post.description: extend the session TTL. Reads session, verifies org ownership, then bumps the TTL by body.ttl_seconds (or a default extension).
mark the session/identity sticky (pinned). Reads session, verifies org ownership, then writes back `pinned=True` + `pinned_at=<epoch>` preserving the TTL.
unpin the session and free the slot. Reads session, verifies org ownership, then writes back `pinned=False` + `released_at=<epoch>`.

POST /v1/sessions/{id}/takeover with {"action": "mint"} delegates to the HMAC-token mint logic (same as /takeover_token). {"action": "release"} delegates to the control flag release logic (same as /takeover_release). with an action discriminator, while the underlying REST surface exposes two dedicated sub-paths. Both entry points share identical auth, tier,
Release takeover control of the session. Requires a paid plan. Returns 200 ``{"released": true}`` on success.
Mint a short-lived session control token for an interactive session.

get.description: owner-scoped cursor pagination.
get.summary: List storage states with cursor pagination

post.description: snapshot browser storage state from a live session. Captures the current cookies, localStorage, and session storage from the named active profile and saves it encrypted under the given name. Returns storage state metadata on success (credentials are never returned). Storage blobs over 5 MB return storage_state_too_large (400).
post.summary: Save storage state from live session

post.description: clear profile sticky-attach binding. Removes the storage state binding from a profile so subsequent sessions start without a pre-loaded storage state. Returns 204.

delete.description: cascade binding via. Returns 204 on success, 404 (storage_state_not_found) on miss.

get.description: metadata only.

post.description: attach a storage state to a profile. Decrypts and validates the named storage state, filters expired cookies, then sets the sticky binding so subsequent sessions on this profile start with the saved state loaded. Corrupted data → storage_state_decrypt_failed (400).

get.description: cursor pagination. without it → 403.

post.description: Create a new webhook subscription. Returns the webhook plus the plaintext signing secret once. Store the secret immediately, it cannot be recovered; only rotation via /rotate-secret provides a new one.

get.description: Secret: NOT returned.
GET /v1/webhooks/{webhook_id}/deliveries.

post.description: POST /v1/webhooks/{webhook_id}/deliveries/{delivery_id}/retry. Re-enqueues the failed delivery with a fresh delivery_id; the original event_id is preserved so downstream consumers can detect the retry.
POST /v1/webhooks/{webhook_id}/rotate-secret. Generate a new signing secret for the webhook. The previous secret remains valid for a short grace period so in-flight deliveries can still be verified.
post.summary: Rotate webhook signing secret (previous secret valid during grace period).
